actions-list.actionItem: The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is subject to the call of the Chair.
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.
On motion that the House suspend the rules and recede from the House amendment to the Senate amendment to H.R. 7147, and concur in the Senate amendment Agreed to by voice vote.

actions-list.header: DEBATE
actions-list.actionItem: The House proceeded with 40 minutes of debate on the motion to suspend the rules and recede from the House amendment to the Senate amendment to H.R. 7147, and concur in the Senate amendment.
Mr. Alford moved that the House suspend the rules and recede from the House amendment to the Senate amendment to H.R. 7147, and concur in the Senate amendment
The Speaker announced that votes on suspensions, if ordered, will be postponed until a time to be announced.

Mr. Alford asked unanimous consent to take from the Speaker's table the bill H.R. 7147, with the House amendment to the Senate amendment thereto, to recede from the House amendment, and to concur in the Senate amendment. Objection heard.
The House convened, returning from a recess continuing the legislative day of April 30.
The Speaker announced that the House do now recess. The next meeting is subject to the call of the Chair.

actions-list.header: ASSUMING FIRST SPONSORSHIP
actions-list.actionItem: Mr. LaHood asked unanimous consent that he may hereafter be considered as the first sponsor of H.R. 2424, a bill originally introduced by Representative LaMalfa, for the purpose of adding cosponsors and requesting reprintings pursuant to clause 7 of rule XII. Agreed to without objection.
The Clerk was authorized to correct section numbers, punctuation, and cross references, and to make other necessary technical and conforming corrections in the engrossment of H.R. 7567.
Motion to reconsider laid on the table Agreed to without objection.

actions-list.header: On passage Passed by the Yeas and Nays: 224
On motion to recommit Failed by the Yeas and Nays: 211

actions-list.actionItem: The previous question on the motion to recommit was ordered pursuant to clause 2(b) of rule XIX.
Ms. Craig moved to recommit to the Committee on Agriculture.
The House adopted the amendments en gros as agreed to by the Committee of the Whole House on the state of the Union.
The previous question was ordered pursuant to the rule.
The House rose from the Committee of the Whole House on the state of the Union to report H.R. 7567.

actions-list.header: On agreeing to the Spartz amendment; Agreed to by recorded vote: 215
On agreeing to the Self amendment; Failed by recorded vote: 186
On agreeing to the Scholten amendment; as modified Agreed to by recorded vote: 233
On agreeing to the Moore (WV) amendment; Failed by recorded vote: 187
On agreeing to the Luna amendment; Agreed to by recorded vote: 280
On agreeing to the Grothman amendment; Failed by recorded vote: 69
On agreeing to the Grothman amendment; Agreed to by recorded vote: 416
actions-list.actionItem: 8 (Roll no. 146).

actions-list.header: On agreeing to the Crawford amendment; Agreed to by recorded vote: 384
actions-list.actionItem: 35 (Roll no. 145).

actions-list.header: On agreeing to the Bentz amendment; Failed by recorded vote: 210

actions-list.actionItem: On agreeing to the Steube amendment; Agreed to by voice vote.

actions-list.header: DEBATE
actions-list.actionItem: Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1224, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 10 minutes of debate on the Steube amendment No. 51.
An amendment, offered by Mr. Steube,  numbered 51 printed in Part B of House Report 119-628 to establish testing standards for honey products
On agreeing to the Steube amendment; Agreed to by voice vote.

actions-list.header: DEBATE
actions-list.actionItem: Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1224, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 10 minutes of debate on the Steube amendment No. 50.
An amendment, offered by Mr. Steube,  numbered 50 printed in Part B of House Report 119-628 to prohibit the purchase of agricultural land by foreign adversaries and state sponsors of terrorism

actions-list.header: POSTPONED PROCEEDINGS
actions-list.actionItem: At the conclusion of debate on the Spartz amendment No. 49, the Chair put the question on agreeing to the amendment and by voice vote, announced the ayes had prevailed. Ms. Craig demanded a recorded vote, and the Chair postponed further proceedings until a time to be announced.

actions-list.header: DEBATE
actions-list.actionItem: Pursuant to the provisions of H. Res. 1224, the Committee of the Whole proceeded with 10 minutes of debate on the Spartz amendment No. 49.

An amendment, offered by Mrs. Spartz,  numbered 49 printed in Part B of House Report 119-628 to remove costly, overreaching emissions mandates on farm equipment that drives up expenses for farmers and consumers.
The House resolved into Committee of the Whole House on the state of the Union for further consideration.

Considered as unfinished business. H.R. 7567 — "To provide for the reform and continuation of agricultural and other programs of the Department of Agriculture through fiscal year 2031, and for other purposes."

actions-list.header: Commission on the National Defense Strategy
actions-list.actionItem: Pursuant to section 1095(b)(1)(A) of the National Defense Authorization Act for fiscal year 2026 (Public Law 119-60), and the order of the House of January 3, 2025, the Speaker appointed Mr. Robert O'Brien, Heber City, Utah.

actions-list.header: Advisory Board for the House of Representatives Child Care Center
actions-list.actionItem: Pursuant to 2 U.S.C. 2062, and the order of the House of January 3, 2025, the Speaker appointed Mr. John Byers, Alexandria, VA; Ms. Rachel Collins, Washington, DC; Ms. Giulia Leganski, Potomac, MD; Mr. Derek Harley, Avon Lake, Ohio; Ms. Lauren Hodge, Alexandria, VA; Mr. Thomas McAllister, Washington, DC; Ms. Anna McCormack, Alexandria, VA; and Mr. Kevin O'Keefe, Falls Church, VA.

actions-list.header: ONE MINUTE SPEECHES
actions-list.actionItem: The House proceeded with one minute speeches which by direction of the Chair, would be limited to 5 per side of the aisle.

actions-list.header: PLEDGE OF ALLEGIANCE
actions-list.actionItem: The Chair designated Mr. Morelle to lead the Members in reciting the Pledge of Allegiance to the Flag.
The Speaker announced approval of the Journal.  Pursuant to clause 1, rule I, the Journal stands approved.
Today's prayer was offered by Rabbi Peter Stein, Temple B'rith Kodesh, Rochester, New York
The House convened, starting a new legislative day.
